title: Filter 'not found' messages when parsing CLI output
problem: When parsing CLI tool output programmatically, 'No results found' or similar messages intended for human consumption get incorrectly parsed as data entries. In sg context, 'No runes found' was being parsed as a rune entry with ID='No' and Title='- runes found'.
solution: Add filter condition to skip lines containing 'not found' or similar empty-result messages before parsing. Use strings.HasPrefix() or strings.Contains() to detect these messages. Example: if strings.HasPrefix(line, 'Found') || line == '' || strings.HasPrefix(line, 'Problem:') || strings.HasPrefix(line, 'Tags:') || strings.HasPrefix(line, 'No runes found') { continue }
learned: CLI tools often output human-readable 'not found' messages that look like data entries. Always filter these before parsing structured output.
